summary: Performance measurement systems are clinically valuable because they improve risk detection, enhance guideline adherence, increase transparency, reduce avoidable complications like rehospitalizations, and boost patient satisfaction through continuous feedback loops grounded in robust observational data. The consistent improvement observed in outcome measures supports their effectiveness in improving quality-of-care delivery within complex health environments.
